twitter: 🤯 Our 'AI expert' friends said voice AI wasn't ready... turns out it's been available for MONTHS. Also discovered building serious AI needs 140GB+ RAM. The gap between hype and reality is wild. New @TwoBrooklynGuys episode drops insights 🧵 [LINK]
linkedin:
The AI development reality check we didn't expect 🎯

In our latest Two Brooklyn Guys episode, we discovered some hard truths about the AI landscape. While 'experts' told us voice AI wasn't ready, we found it's been available and working for months. The real shocker? Building serious AI applications requires massive hardware investments - potentially 140GB+ RAM for large language models.

We also realized the AI sales agent market is already crowded with established players like Conversica and SalesLoft. It's a reminder that in the AI space, things move incredibly fast, and yesterday's impossibilities are today's basic features. For entrepreneurs and developers, the lesson is clear: don't rely on secondhand expertise in such a rapidly evolving field.

What's your experience with the gap between AI hype and actual capabilities? [LINK]
threads:
Just dropped a new episode where we learned some wild stuff about AI development 🤯

Turns out our 'expert' friends saying voice AI isn't ready were completely wrong - it's been available for months lol

But the real kicker? If you want to run serious AI models, you need 140GB+ of RAM. Like, what?? 😅

The hardware requirements are absolutely insane. We're talking about potentially needing to spend thousands just on RAM alone.

Also realized the AI sales agent space is already packed with established companies. So much for being early to the party 😂

Anyway, check it out if you're curious about what it actually takes to build AI stuff beyond the hype [LINK]
